message:
fix(http): treat 1xx/204/205/304 responses as empty-body per RFC 9110

HTTP status codes 1xx, 204 (No Content), 205 (Reset Content), and 304
(Not Modified) must never carry a message body regardless of headers.
Previously the client inferred body framing solely from headers, so a
keep-alive 204 with no Content-Length would fall through to EOF-delimited
mode and block until the server closed the connection or the timeout
fired.

Add `body_kind_from_response()` that short-circuits to BodyKind::Empty
for these status codes before consulting headers. This lets the client
return immediately for bodiless responses on persistent connections.

Includes unit tests for 204, 205, and 304 status handling plus an
integration test that verifies a 204 on a keep-alive connection returns
an empty body without waiting for socket close.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
